contest.title: 2025-2026 ICPC, NERC, Northern Eurasia Finals (Unrated, Online Mirror, ICPC Rules, Teams Preferred)

sources.notes: Official NERC tutorial; used to confirm the Bayesian posterior DP and the endpoint-bet optimization.

reference_candidates.notes: 洛谷题解候选来源；只有实际参考后才移动到 sources。

annotation.summary: A gambler has a hidden win probability selected from a known finite set. After observing wins/losses, choose bets to maximize expected final money after k rounds.
annotation.constraints: k is small enough for a DP over observed win/loss counts, while money values should not be part of the state explicitly.
annotation.core_idea: Given a history with a wins and b losses, use Bayes' rule to compute the posterior probability that the next round is a win. Let f[a][b] be the best expected multiplier for one unit of current money from that state. The recurrence is linear in the chosen bet fraction y: p*f[a+1][b]*(1+y)+(1-p)*f[a][b+1]*(1-y). A linear objective over y in [0,1] is maximized at an endpoint, so the optimal action is either bet nothing or all current money. This removes the money dimension and leaves an O(k^2 * n) posterior DP.
annotation.complexity: O(k^2 n) after precomputing posterior sums over n candidate probabilities.
tricks: expected value is homogeneous in current money

solution_variants.name: main
solution_variants.summary: DP over (wins, losses) storing best expected multiplier; compute posterior win probabilities from the prior list and choose all-in or no-bet by the sign of a linear coefficient.

tags.evidence: The recurrence optimizes expected future money over the number of observed wins and losses.
The next-win probability is recomputed from the posterior distribution after a wins and b losses.
tags.description: Use Bayes' rule or posterior weights to update probabilities after observations.
tags.created_reason: Posterior-update probability DP is distinct from generic probability and expectation tags.

tags.evidence: The optimized bet appears linearly in the recurrence, so the optimum is always at y=0 or y=1.
tags.description: When a decision variable appears only linearly over an interval, reduce the choice to one of the interval endpoints.
tags.created_reason: This endpoint-choice optimization is a reusable trick in expected value and greedy problems.

tags.evidence: The whole recurrence is written in terms of conditional probabilities and expectation.
